description_long: This vase features a bulbous, rounded body with a narrow cylindrical neck extending outward, giving it a distinctive, almost pitcher-like silhouette. The surface is predominantly a reddish-brown clay color, with visible darkened areas near the opening, suggesting soot or burn marks, possibly from use or age. The mouth of the vase is wide and open, with a slightly irregular rim, and the interior appears dark, hinting at depth and hollowness. Two thin, metallic rods or wires protrude from the back, possibly remnants of a mounting or decorative feature. The exterior texture is rough and matte, with visible imperfections and a hand-crafted appearance. This object evokes a rustic, utilitarian aesthetic, suitable for outdoor or urban decorative use.
description: A rustic, reddish-brown clay vase with a wide mouth, cylindrical neck, and protruding metal rods, featuring a rough, matte surface and visible burn marks.
description_view_0: Front view shows the wide, darkened mouth and cylindrical neck, emphasizing the opening and burn marks.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the bulbous body, the full length of the neck, and the protruding metal rods at the back.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the rounded back of the vase and the two metal rods extending outward.
description_view_3: Opposite side view reveals the vase's profile, showing the neck, body curvature, and metal rods from a different angle.
